rows.design_decision: Applying 2D Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) on 9x9 angular samples to analyze frequency domain energy distribution for material classification.
rows.motivation: Traditional methods fail to distinguish complex reflection types (diffuse, specular, scattering) from limited angular samples, causing non-Lambertian processing to fail.
rows.sota_gap: Unlike heavy CNNs extracting spatial-angular features, frequency analysis provides explicit physical meaning and achieves O(HW) complexity for lightweight, real-time pixel-level classification.
rows.scenario: Mixed material and complex illumination conditions, specifically evaluated on the Non-lambertian_zhenglong dataset.
rows.evidence: Phase 1 validation criteria: 3-class material classification accuracy >85% and AUC >0.90; implemented in `models/angle_fft.py`.

rows.design_decision: Constructing a Dual-Mask Physical Model (Media Mask for surface roughness and Angular Mask for deflection vector fields) based on the Radiance Tensor Field (RTF) rank assumption.
rows.motivation: Pure data-driven models lack physical interpretability and cannot uniformly model light-matter interactions across Lambertian, Non-Lambertian, and Mixed scenes.
rows.sota_gap: Overcomes black-box limitations by fitting 81 angular equations via constrained least squares, outputting physically meaningful BRDF weights (diffuse, specular, scattering) instead of arbitrary latent features.
rows.scenario: Mixed scenes requiring differentiation between scattering and specular reflections caused by varying surface roughness.
rows.evidence: Phase 2 validation criteria: BRDF parameter weight estimation error <10% on synthetic data; implemented in `models/dual_mask_depth.py`.

rows.design_decision: Decoupling depth estimation into diffuse, specular, and scattering components, applying tailored strategies (EPI, photometric stereo/neighborhood propagation, scattering correction) and weighted fusion.
rows.motivation: Traditional EPI methods fatally degenerate into texture copying in non-Lambertian regions due to the blind reliance on the photo-consistency assumption.
rows.sota_gap: Completely resolves the fatal flaw of single-strategy EPI (which yields an MAE of 0.411 in Non-Lambertian domains), significantly improving robustness via component-specific estimation.
rows.scenario: Non-Lambertian scenes containing highly reflective or strongly scattering materials (Non-lambertian_zhenglong dataset).
rows.evidence: Key finding showing baseline EPI MAE=0.411 and texture copying in Non-Lambertian domains; expected significant MAE reduction post-fusion.

rows.design_decision: Introducing a pixel-level confidence weighting mechanism in the loss function, driven by the Media Mask weights.
rows.motivation: Gradients from non-Lambertian regions (e.g., specular highlights, strong scattering) severely interfere with overall network convergence, degrading depth accuracy in Lambertian areas.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to global MSE or L1 loss, confidence weighting effectively suppresses gradient interference from non-Lambertian outliers, protecting the optimization of Lambertian regions.
rows.scenario: Training phase, particularly on complex mixed scenes with large non-Lambertian areas (Non-lambertian_zhenglong).
rows.evidence: Ablation study comparing convergence speed and final MAE/BadPix metrics between weighted loss and standard L1 loss on mixed scenes.
rows.section: Methodology - Loss Function / Experiments - Ablation Study

rows.design_decision: Designing an ultra-lightweight network architecture (~108K parameters) that heavily relies on physical priors (frequency analysis and RTF rank constraints) rather than deep CNN stacking.
rows.motivation: Light field data is high-dimensional (5D); traditional 3D/4D CNNs have massive parameters, easily overfitting on limited light field datasets and exhibiting poor generalization.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to million-parameter models like EPINet or LFNet, this architecture replaces data fitting with physical priors, reducing parameters by >90% while accelerating inference.
rows.scenario: Resource-constrained deployment environments or real-time 3D reconstruction tasks, and scenarios with limited training data (e.g., HCInew with only 20 training scenes).
rows.evidence: Model parameter statistics (~108K) comparison; inference time (FPS) and GPU memory footprint benchmarks on HCInew and Non-lambertian_zhenglong.

rows.design_decision: Extracting 4-direction Epipolar Plane Image (EPI) features (horizontal, vertical, main/sub-diagonals) combined with confidence masks for initial disparity regression.
rows.motivation: Single-direction EPI is susceptible to occlusion and edge blurring, failing to fully utilize the multi-dimensional geometric structure of the light field.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to methods using only horizontal/vertical EPIs, 4-direction EPI with confidence masks handles weak textures and occlusion edges more robustly, providing higher-quality initial depth priors.
rows.scenario: Lambertian scenes with rich geometric edges and occlusion relationships (HCInew dataset) and diffuse regions in Non-Lambertian scenes.
rows.evidence: BadPix(0.07) metric comparison on HCInew; ablation study showing performance differences between 2-direction and 4-direction EPI extraction.
rows.section: Methodology - LF Input & EPI Extraction / Experiments
